01-eratosthenes: Calculate the circumference of the Earth from the difference in shadow angles between two cities.
02-archimedes-buoyancy: Observe the principle that an object experiences a buoyant force equal to the weight of the water it displaces.
03-archimedes-lever: Explore the equilibrium of a lever that amplifies force through the fulcrum and distance.
04-ptolemy-copernicus: Compare planetary retrograde motion using the epicycle model and the heliocentric model.
01-kepler: Simulate elliptical orbits and the law of equal areas in equal times.
02-galileo-freefall: Confirm that all objects fall with the same acceleration regardless of mass.
03-galileo-incline: Measure acceleration from the relationship between distance and the square of time on an inclined plane.
04-galileo-telescope: Observe Jupiter's moons and the Moon's surface through a telescope, transforming astronomy.
05-newton-prism: See white light pass through a prism and split into a rainbow spectrum.
06-newton-cannon: Show that increasing launch speed turns a cannonball into a satellite orbiting the Earth.
07-newton-gravitation: Explore the attractive force between two masses that is inversely proportional to the square of the distance.
01-boyle: Measure how a gas's pressure and volume are inversely proportional at constant temperature.
02-cavendish: Measure a tiny attractive force with a torsion balance to weigh the Earth.
03-coulomb: See with a torsion balance that the force between charges is inversely proportional to the square of the distance.
04-volta: Create a continuous electric current from different metals and an electrolyte.
05-young-double-slit: Prove the wave nature of light through the interference pattern of light passing through two slits.
01-foucault: Directly demonstrate the Earth's rotation through the rotation of a pendulum's plane of oscillation.
02-joule: Measure how much work is converted into heat as a falling weight stirs water.
03-carnot: Explore the maximum efficiency of an ideal heat engine and the Carnot cycle.
04-faraday: Observe how a changing magnetic field induces a current in a coil.
05-maxwell: Unify electricity and magnetism and predict that light is an electromagnetic wave.
06-hertz: Generate and detect electromagnetic waves via spark discharge, proving Maxwell right.
07-boltzmann: See the velocity distribution of countless particles and the statistical meaning of entropy.
01-michelson-morley: Fail to find the ether with an interferometer and instead discover the invariance of the speed of light.
02-roentgen: Discover that unknown rays emitted from a cathode-ray tube can penetrate objects.
03-thomson: Deflect cathode rays with electric and magnetic fields to measure the electron's charge-to-mass ratio.
04-planck: Explain the blackbody radiation spectrum through the quantization of energy.
05-photoelectric: See the particle nature of light as electrons are emitted depending on the frequency of the light.
06-millikan: Measure the elementary charge from the equilibrium of oil droplets in an electric field.
07-rutherford: Discover the existence of the atomic nucleus through alpha-particle scattering.
08-bohr: See the relationship between an electron's quantized orbits and spectral lines.
09-franck-hertz: Prove the quantization of atomic energy levels through the energy steps of electron collisions.
01-de-broglie: Visualize the concept of matter waves, that particles also have a wavelength.
02-davisson-germer: Experimentally confirm matter waves through the diffraction pattern of electrons.
03-schrodinger: Explore the time evolution of the wave function and its probability distribution.
04-uncertainty: See the limit that position and momentum cannot both be known precisely at the same time.
05-stern-gerlach: See spin quantization as a beam of silver atoms splits into two branches.
06-quantum-tunneling: See the phenomenon of a particle passing through a barrier it classically could not surmount.
07-bell-inequality: Show that quantum entanglement violates local hidden-variable theories.
01-time-dilation: See the special-relativistic effect in which a fast-moving clock runs slow.
02-length-contraction: Visualize the effect in which an object's length contracts along its direction of motion.
03-spacetime-curvature: See general relativity, in which mass curves spacetime to create gravity.
04-eddington: Verify general relativity by measuring the bending of starlight during a solar eclipse.
05-gravitational-lensing: See the phenomenon in which a huge mass bends the light of a background object to form images.
06-hubble: Show that the universe is expanding through the redshift of galaxies.
07-cmb: Discover the afterglow of the Big Bang in the microwave noise across the entire sky.
01-laser: See the process by which coherent light is produced through stimulated emission and optical amplification.
02-superconductivity: See the phenomenon in which resistance drops to zero and a magnet levitates below the critical temperature.
03-nuclear-fission: See the process by which neutrons split heavy nuclei and a chain reaction spreads.
04-gravitational-waves: Detect with an interferometer the ripples in spacetime created by a black-hole collision.
05-black-hole-eht: Link radio telescopes across the entire globe to image a black hole's shadow.
06-higgs-boson: See the Higgs field that gives particles mass and the detection of its particle.
07-qubit: See the operating principle of a qubit that uses superposition and entanglement.
08-single-photon-eraser: Send photons one at a time so the interference pattern builds up dot by dot; the pattern vanishes when which-path information is obtained and is restored by the eraser.
09-hydrogen-orbitals: See how the shape of the electron probability cloud (orbital) changes according to the principal quantum number n, orbital quantum number ℓ, and magnetic quantum number m.
01-bloch-sphere: View a qubit state as a point on a sphere and rotate it with the X, Y, Z, H, S, and T gates to understand superposition and phase.
02-quantum-circuit: Place gates in a circuit to see the amplitudes and probabilities of 2-3 qubit states in real time, and collapse them by measurement.
03-grover: Amplify the amplitude of the marked answer at each iteration (amplitude amplification) to find it among N unsorted items in about √N steps.
04-entanglement-bell: Entangle two qubits with Hadamard + CNOT to create a Bell state, and see the correlation in which measuring one instantly determines the other.
10-harmonic-oscillator: See the evenly spaced energy levels of a particle in a parabolic potential, the wave functions ψₙ, and the oscillation of a coherent state.
11-particle-in-box: See the quantized energy levels Eₙ ∝ n² and standing-wave wave functions of a particle confined in an infinite well.
12-wave-packet: A Gaussian wave packet spreads as it moves — see the difference between group velocity and phase velocity.
05-teleportation: Use entanglement and 2 bits of classical communication to transmit an unknown qubit state elsewhere without copying it.
06-bb84: Share a secret key with polarized photons; if an eavesdropper intervenes, the error rate spikes and they are detected.
07-error-correction: Encode one qubit into three to find and correct bit-flip errors through syndrome measurement.
08-deutsch-jozsa: The first quantum speedup, distinguishing whether a function is constant or balanced with just a single query.
09-qft: A transform that embeds periodic information into the phases of basis states — the core engine of Shor's algorithm.
10-shor: Find the period of modular exponentiation with the QFT to factor large numbers in polynomial time.
11-quantum-walk: Through the superposition and interference of coin and position, a distribution emerges that is entirely different from a classical random walk.
12-quantum-supremacy: Sample the output distribution of a random quantum circuit — a task practically impossible to reproduce on a classical computer.
